Added Seomyeon Hound Hotel 1st Street (Busan 18Apr) to the hotel plan with the THB 2,189 Agoda rate + photo set. Also compared Jeju→Busan (Jeju Air 7C503 THB 1,095) vs Jeju→Fukuoka (best seen ~$104/THB 3,600 on Expedia) so we can judge the value/immigration trade-off.

TRIP NOTES canonical dates confirmed by Trent: APRIL 9–19 (Asia/Bangkok). Ensure all planning outputs echo these dates to prevent drift.

Trent schedule reality: irregular during break. Regular classes Tue+Fri 17:30–19:30 (Bangkok). Usually offline 16:30–20:30 those days. Generally awake until ~00:00 but prefers earlier sleep; avoid non-urgent pings late night—leave notes instead.
TRIP NOTES (Latest plan): HK 9–11; Osaka 11–16 (stay Osaka on 14); Busan transit 16; Jeju 16–18; Busan 18–19; return 19. Hiroshima by JR (Kansai–Hiroshima pass). JR pass THB 3,331.

cycle: CHANGED: Jobs panel now shows a timestamp (“HH:MM · LEVEL”) and lightly color-tints the LEVEL by severity (error/needs_approval/question/answer) while keeping the same calm 3-line feed.  
WHY: You can scan “when + what kind of thing” instantly (freshness + urgency) without adding noise or extra UI.  
NEXT: Add a tiny “Filter: unread only” toggle (client-side) so the panel can stay ultra-quiet when you’ve already cleared/seen everything.

cycle: CHANGED: Updated `mission-control.html` so tapping a `runAction()` button disables that button and changes its label to “Running…” until the request finishes (then restores it).
WHY: Prevents double-taps / duplicate runs and makes “did it register?” unambiguous—small reliability + UX win with low risk.
NEXT: Add a lightweight timeout indicator (e.g., “still running (10s)…” after 10s) so long jobs don’t look stalled.
